title: The Last Message Online
content:
The message appeared on an old social media account that had not been active for years.

'Hope you're doing well. It's been a long time.'

The sender was a friend Marcus had known during university. They had once spoken every day, sharing plans, frustrations, and ambitions. Life eventually carried them in different directions. Careers, responsibilities, and distance gradually replaced regular conversations.

Seeing the message triggered a wave of memories. Marcus spent the evening scrolling through photographs, conversations, and posts preserved by platforms that seemed to archive fragments of entire lives.

Technology had created an unusual form of permanence. Moments that might once have faded with time remained stored indefinitely, waiting to be rediscovered years later.

Marcus replied. The conversation began cautiously but soon regained a familiar rhythm. They exchanged updates, laughed about old stories, and promised to stay in touch.

The last message online had become the first message of a renewed friendship. It served as a reminder that behind every username, profile picture, and archived conversation exists a real person carrying memories, experiences, and connections worth preserving.
